items.correct: 12 — the product of two negatives is positive.
items.misconception: -12, because there's a negative sign in the calculation.
items.random_wrong: -7, because the negatives are being combined.
items.why: Students preserve negative signs out of habit and don't internalise that an even number of negative factors produces a positive product.

items.q: What is the formula for the area of a circle?
items.correct: pi * r^2 (pi times the radius squared).
items.misconception: The area of a circle is 2 * pi * r — the same as the circumference formula.
items.random_wrong: The area of a circle is pi * d, where d is the diameter.
items.why: Students remember 'pi and r' for circles but mix up the area formula with the circumference, since both involve pi and r.

items.q: What does the graph of y = 1/x do as x approaches 0?
items.correct: y goes to plus or minus infinity — the graph has a vertical asymptote at x = 0.
items.misconception: y equals 0 because dividing 1 by something small is small.
items.random_wrong: y equals 1 because the numerator stays the same.
items.why: Students confuse 'small denominator gives small result' (true for normal fractions) with 'small denominator gives huge result' (true for 1/x near 0).
Correct answer: 4. “4.8” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which average is most affected by extreme outliers?
items.why: Correct answer: mean. “median” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The real figure is 8. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
